key_insight: Training-free dimensional adaptation plus ratio-controlled weighted averaging can merge heterogeneous LLM checkpoints surprisingly well, but only when the interpolation ratio is carefully kept away from a near-balanced mix.
engineering_relevance: For practical model fusion, simple parameter averaging is a strong low-complexity baseline that can transfer complementary capabilities across different billion-parameter models without distillation, adapters, or semantic alignment.
method: Expand the smaller model into the larger parameter space for union-style merging.
Truncate the larger model into the smaller parameter space for intersection-style merging.
Apply deterministic weighted interpolation with carefully chosen ratios to preserve source behavior and sometimes improve performance.
evidence_signal: The authors evaluate Qwen-family model pairs on benchmarks spanning mathematical reasoning, code generation, language understanding, commonsense reasoning, knowledge, and instruction following, reporting both gains and collapse cases.
watch_item: Near-balanced interpolation can collapse and task-level gains may come with regressions elsewhere, so ratio selection is critical and merging may induce a seesaw trade-off.
paper_title: Rethinking Heterogeneous LLM Merging: A Weighted Model Averaging Perspective
